article.body_preview:
DHAKA, Feb 28, 2023 (BSS) - Metropolitan Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MCCI) has observed that Bangladesh's economy has been showing some signs of improvement.

In a report titled 'Review of Economic Situation in Bangladesh October - December 2022 (Q2 of FY23)', MCCI today mentioned that Bangladesh's robust economic recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic has been interrupted by the war in Russia-Ukraine, resultant supply-chain disruptions, global oil-and food-price spikes, slowdown in external

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Russia-Ukraine, resultant supply-chain disruptions, global oil-and food-price spikes, slowdown in external demand, weak remittance inflow, rise in inflation, negative current account balance, depreciation of the Taka
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly attributes 'depreciation of the Taka' to effects from the Russia-Ukraine war, which matches the feature's scope describing USD/BDT exchange-rate movement.

verification.quote_best_match: Russia-Ukraine, resultant supply-chain disruptions, global oil-and food-price spikes, slowdown in external demand, weak remittance inflow, rise in inflation, negative current account balance, depreciation of the Taka
verification.judge_reasoning: The article text explicitly lists the Russia-Ukraine war and resultant disruptions as interrupting Bangladesh's recovery and then enumerates consequences including 'depreciation of the Taka,' directly linking the war/effects to currency depreciation at the country level.
